full_prompt: Design an aluminum extrusion curtain-wall frame that supports a single rectangular insulating glass unit (IGU) bonded with a continuous bead of high-strength acrylic-foam structural glazing tape (G23F-class) on all four sides, under wind and dead loads, following the structural-glazing-tape design guide and ASTM C1184. The glass unit is 1,500 mm x 2,400 mm x 28 mm IGU (6 mm outer + 16 mm argon spacer + 6 mm inner tempered per ASTM E2190) weighing approximately 106 kg (26 kg/m^2 x 3.6 m^2). The aluminum frame is 6063-T5 structural extrusion (yield 170 MPa, UTS 205 MPa, Young's modulus 68.9 GPa, density 2700 kg/m^3), 75 mm box profile with 3 mm wall thickness, welded at the corners. The structural-glazing-tape bond is applied as a continuous 12 mm wide x 3 mm thick bead around the IGU perimeter (total bonded length ~= 7,800 mm). Design stress data for the high-strength acrylic-foam structural glazing tape (G23F-class) per the structural-glazing-tape design guide (typical published values): ultimate tensile adhesion 620 kPa, allowable design tensile stress 103 kPa (ASTM C1184 SF = 6 on ultimate), allowable design shear stress 70 kPa, dynamic multiplier 1.0 for static dead load and 1.3 for peak wind gust (glazing-tape design guide Table 3). Design load cases: LC1 dead load = 106 kg x 9.81 = 1,040 N down, shared on lower bond line; LC2 positive wind pressure 1.5 kPa (design wind for 50 m/s regional 3-second gust x appropriate C_p per ASCE 7-22) x 3.6 m^2 = 5,400 N tensile into the bond; LC3 negative wind (suction) 2.4 kPa x 3.6 m^2 = 8,640 N tensile; LC4 seismic in-plane shear at 0.3 g = 0.3 * 1,040 = 312 N in-plane. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC3 (worst tensile case), the average tensile stress on the structural-glazing-tape bond area (perimeter x 12 mm width = 93,600 mm^2 = 0.0936 m^2) is 8,640 / 0.0936 = 92.3 kPa, which must be <= allowable design tensile 103 kPa and also <= ultimate / SF 6 = 103 kPa; (R2) under LC2, short-term dynamic multiplied 1.3 x 92.3 * (5400/8640) = 75 kPa must stay <= 103 kPa; (R3) under LC1 + LC4 static dead + seismic shear, combined shear on bond <= allowable design shear 70 kPa; (R4) under LC3, aluminum frame max von Mises <= 170 / 1.65 = 103 MPa (AA ADM 2020 allowable stress design SF on 6063-T5); (R5) frame deflection perpendicular to glass plane under LC3 <= L/175 = 2400/175 = 13.7 mm (ASTM E1300 + AAMA TIR-A11 glass deflection limit); (R6) minimum structural-glazing-tape bead width 12 mm and minimum bonded contact length per ASTM C1401 structural sealant design shall not be reduced below 9 mm in any location around the perimeter. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the aluminum frame extrusion assembly plus a glazing-bond layout drawing; report with per-side bond tensile/shear stress, aluminum frame max von Mises, perpendicular deflection, and a compliance statement vs ASTM C1184 + structural-glazing-tape allowables.
short_prompt:
Design an aluminum-extrusion curtain-wall frame that supports a single rectangular IGU bonded with a continuous bead of high-strength acrylic-foam structural glazing tape (G23F-class) on all four sides, per the structural-glazing-tape design guide and ASTM C1184.

Geometry: glass unit 1,500 mm x 2,400 mm x 28 mm IGU (6 mm outer + 16 mm argon spacer + 6 mm inner tempered per ASTM E2190), ~106 kg. Aluminum frame in 6063-T5 extrusion, 75 mm box profile with 3 mm walls, welded at corners. Structural-glazing-tape bead: 12 mm wide x 3 mm thick, continuous around the perimeter (total bonded length ~7,800 mm).

Design stress data (G23F-class): ultimate tensile adhesion 620 kPa, allowable design tensile 103 kPa (ASTM C1184 SF = 6), allowable design shear 70 kPa, dynamic multiplier 1.0 (dead) / 1.3 (peak gust).

Load cases.
- LC1 dead load: 1,040 N shared on the lower bond line.
- LC2 positive wind: 1.5 kPa x 3.6 m^2 = 5,400 N tensile into the bond.
- LC3 negative wind (suction): 2.4 kPa x 3.6 m^2 = 8,640 N tensile (worst tensile).
- LC4 seismic in-plane shear: 0.3 g x 1,040 = 312 N in-plane.

Frame checks: aluminum max von Mises under LC3 <= 103 MPa (AA ADM 2020); deflection perpendicular to glass <= L/175 = 13.7 mm (ASTM E1300 + AAMA TIR-A11).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: ASTM E1300 glass deflection/strength is a tabulated closed-form method.
ASCE 7-22 C_p, K_z, K_zt, and gust factor are tabulated code quantities.

prompt.title: Aluminum curtain-wall frame with high-strength acrylic-foam structural glazing tape bonding a 1.5 x 2.4 m IGU
prompt.brief: Bond a 28 mm IGU (1500 x 2400 mm) to a 6063-T5 aluminum box frame via continuous 12 x 3 mm high-strength acrylic-foam structural glazing tape (G23F-class) bead. Survive dead + design wind + seismic within ASTM C1184 SF 6 and AA ADM frame limits.

boundary_conditions.frame_to_slab_edge: mechanical clips, fully constrained at building anchor
boundary_conditions.glass_to_frame: continuous structural-glazing-tape bond, 12x3 mm around perimeter

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: ultimate 620 kPa / SF 6 per ASTM C1184 + structural-glazing-tape design guide
170 / 1.65 per AA ADM 2020 allowable stress design for 6063-T5
L/175 with L=2400 mm per ASTM E1300 + AAMA TIR-A11
ASTM C1401 minimum retained contact width

requirements.governing_standard: ASTM C1184 + ASTM C1401 + ASCE 7-22 + AA ADM 2020 + structural-glazing-tape Design Guide 2023

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Out of scope for static curtain-wall brief; wind-induced fatigue is a separate variant.
exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Thermal-cycle differential expansion between glass and aluminum is a known secondary concern but is out of scope here.

notes.numeric_source: High-strength acrylic-foam structural glazing tape (G23F-class) Design Guide 2023; ASTM C1184 SF 6 on ultimate; ASCE 7-22 pressures typical for ASCE Exposure B in 50 m/s zones.

eval_coverage: allowable stress design against ASTM code
aluminum frame + continuous bond coupled analysis
